data.title: Intuitive Machines slips on launch of public offering and concurrent private placement
data.description:
• Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) has commenced an underwritten public offering of $65M of shares of its class A common stock.
  • The company and a selling stockholder intend to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $8.88M and $877,500 of shares of Class A Common Stock from the company and such selling stockholder, respectively.
  • On December 2, 2024, the company also entered into an agreement with Boryung Corporation, together with its affiliates, to sell to Boryung $10M of shares of class A common stock in a concurrent private placement at a purchase price per share equal to the public offering price per share in the offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds it receives from the offering and the private placement, together with its existing cash, cash equivalents and short-term investment balance, to acquire an equivalent number of newly-issued common units of Intuitive Machines, LLC from Intuitive Machines OpCo.
  • LUNR shares fell 14.8% in after hours trading on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Genpact signs collaboration agreement with AWS to accelerate AI adoption
data.description:
• Genpact (NYSE:G) announced a multi-year collaboration agreement with Amazon Web Services (AWS) with plans to accelerate the adoption of AI.
  • By simplifying access to cutting-edge data and AI technologies, Genpact and AWS are allowing a broad range of decision-makers to drive innovation within their organizations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pure Storage surges as Q3 results, guidance top Wall Street estimates
data.description:
Pure Storage (NYSE:PSTG) surged more than 23% in late trading on Tuesday after the data storage company reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's expectations.

For the period ending November 3, Pure Storage said it earned an adjusted $0.50 per share as revenue rose 8.9% year-over-year to come in at $831.1M. While product revenue ticked up just 0.3% year-over-year to come in at $454.7M,subscription services revenue jumped 22% year-over-year to $376.3M during the period.

Subscription annual recurring revenue was $1.6B, while remaining performance obligations rose 16% year-over-year to $2.4B.

Analysts were expecting earnings of $0.42 per share on $814.8M in revenue.

"Pure Storage has achieved another industry first in our journey of data storage innovation with a transformational design win for our DirectFlash technology in a top-four hyperscaler," said Pure Storage Chairman and CEO Charles Giancarlo in a statement. "This win is the vanguard for Pure Flash technology to become the standard for all hyperscaler online storage, providing unparalleled performance and scalability while also reducing operating costs and power consumption."

Looking to the fourth-quarter, Pure Storage expects to generate $867M in revenue, up 9.7% year-over-year and above the $856.9M estimate. Full-year revenue is forecast to rise 11.5% year-over-year to $3.15B, above the $3.13B estimate.

Pure Storage also said it expects an adjusted operating margin of 15.6% and 17% for the fourth-quarter and full-year, respectively.

The company will hold a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Campbell's reports mixed FQ1 results, raises dividend, announces CEO departure
data.description:
Shares of The Campbell’s Company (NASDAQ:CPB) are trending lower in after-hours trading as Wall Street reacts to fiscal Q1 results and the exit of its chief executive, Mark Clouse.

Clouse announced plans to join the National Football League’s Washington Commanders as the team president. He will be replaced by the current president of Meals & Beverages, Mick Beekhuizen on January 31, 2025.

At the same time, the company said it earned a better-than-expected adjusted profit of $0.89 per share on a 10% increase in sales to $2.8B, in line with estimates. Net income was down slightly from a year ago, largely due to cost inflation and other supply chain costs. This impacted the company’s adjusted profit margin, which decreased by 70 basis points to 31.4%. Adjusted EBITDA increased 6% to $432M.

For fiscal 2025, the Camden, NJ-based company reaffirmed its guidance for net sales to increase by 9% to 11% from FY24 which assumes sales of $10.50B to $10.695B, straddling the consensus estimate of $10.54B. Adjusted EPS is expected to be between $3.12 to $3.22, within Wall Street's estimate of $3.19 per share.

Campbell’s (CPB) also raised its quarterly dividend by 2 cents to $0.39 per share, payable on January 27 to shareholders of record as of January 2.

“Our first-quarter results were largely aligned with our expectations. While navigating this dynamic consumer environment and uneven pace of category recovery, we remain agile, focusing on balancing investments and earnings to meet our commitments for this year and the long term,” said Mark Clouse, adding that the company expects both top line and market share sequential improvement in the fiscal second quarter.

Shares are down 4% in after-hours trading.

More on Campbell Soup

  • Hold The Soup: Campbell's Steady Growth Is Fairly Valued
  • Campbell Soup: Hard To Believe Long-Term Growth Targets Can Be Achieved
  • Campbell Soup Q1 2025 Earnings Preview
  • The pantry brand that could be pressuring Olive Garden, Maggiano's and Carrabba's
  • Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on Campbell Soup

data.title: TeraWulf produces 115 bitcoin in November
data.description:
TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) self-mined 115 bitcoin (BTC-USD) in November, a decline from the 150 BTC mined in October, as the company executed a planned outage.

The value per bitcoin self-mined increased to $83,947 from $65,427 in the prior month.

Its average daily production rate slipped to 3.8 bitcoin during the month from 4.8 BTC in the previous month.

The company achieved an average power cost of $41,190 per bitcoin mined, equivalent to ~$0.051/kWh, excluding proceeds from demand response and ancillary services. That compared with average power cost of $36,789 per bitcoin mined in October, equivalent to ~$0.048/kWh.

During the month, TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) completed a planned outage to connect high-voltage redundant power feeds, temporarily affecting ~5.3 EH/s of miners.

"These enhancements will ensure reliable and redundant power delivery to CB-1, CB-2, and future HPC hosting loads, positioning us to meet the increasing demand for Tier 3 HPC infrastructure," said Chief Operating Officer Sean Farrell.

The company said it remains on schedule to deliver 72.5 MW of HPC hosting capacity by the end of Q2 2025.

data.title: Marvell pops as AI helps spur strong Q3 results, guidance
data.description:
Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) rose 8% in late trading on Tuesday after the Mark Murphy-led semiconductor reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, due in part to artificial intelligence.

For the period ending November 2, Marvell said it earned an adjusted $0.43 per share as revenue rose 6.9% year-over-year to come in at $1.52B. Data center revenue accounted for the vast majority of the $1.52B, as sales from that segment rose 98% year-over-year to come in at $1.1B, ahead of the $1.05B estimate. Consumer revenue tumbled 43% year-over-year to $96.5, while carrier infrastructure revenue also plummeted to $84.7M, down 73% year-over-year.

Sales from enterprise networking and automotive and industrial were $150.9M and $82.9M in the period, respectively.

Analysts were expecting $0.41 per share in adjusted earnings and $1.45B in sales.

Shares of Broadcom (AVGO), which competes with Marvell to make chips for other companies in the application-specific integrated circuit market, were up 0.8% in late trading.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, Marvell expects revenue to be $1.8B, plus or minus 5%. Gross margins and adjusted gross margins are expected to be approximately 50% and 60%, while adjusted earnings are forecast to be $0.59 per share, plus or minus $0.05.

Analysts were expecting $0.52 per share in adjusted earnings and $1.65B in revenue.

"Marvell's fiscal third quarter 2025 revenue grew 19% sequentially, well above the mid-point of our guidance, driven by strong demand from AI," said Murphy in a statement. "For the fourth quarter, we are forecasting another 19% sequential revenue growth at the midpoint of guidance, while year-over-year, we expect revenue growth to accelerate significantly to 26%, marking the beginning of a new era of growth for Marvell."

The company will hold a conference call at 4:45 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Rubicon announces strategic refinancing transaction and equity raise
data.description:
• Rubicon Technologies (OTCPK:RBTC) announced, on Tuesday, a new refinancing of certain of Rubicon’s existing debt facilities with proceeds of a new revolving facility and a $20M preferred equity raise.
  • Rodina Capital, a private investment firm based in Florida, led the strategic refinancing and equity raise.

data.title: FTAI Aviation announces sale of offshore energy vessels
data.description:
• FTAI Aviation (NASDAQ:FTAI) on Tuesday, announced the sale of its remaining offshore energy vessels, the Pioneer and the Pride, for approximately $143 million.
  • FTAI said it is continuing to focus on its core business of providing power for the aftermarket of the two largest commercial engine programs, the CFM56 and the V2500.
  • FTAI Aviation (FTAI) shares were up more than 4.2% in post-market trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Sigma Lithium reports record shipment of green lithium to Abu Dhabi firm
data.description:
Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) +2.5% post-market Tuesday after saying it is in the process of loading 27,500 metric tons of its Quintuple Zero Green Lithium for shipment from Brazil to IRH Global Trading in Abu Dhabi.

Sigma Lithium (SGML) said it achieved continuous production of more than 850 metric tons/day of lithium oxide for several consecutive days this month, reaching peak days of 900 tons/day, demonstrating enhanced production capabilities of the Greentech Industrial Plant; as a result, the company said the annualized production run rate reached full capacity of 270K tons/year, a level it expects to maintain going forward.

"Our ability to maintain a consistent monthly shipment cadence is a clear reflection of our operational excellence, and reliability as a mature producer," CEO Ana Cabral said, and with Phase 1 now operating at full capacity, "we are working relentlessly to replicate this industrial success as we move forward with the construction of our second Greentech Industrial Production Plant."

data.title: Torrid GAAP EPS of -$0.01 misses by $0.05, revenue of $263.8M misses by $20.49M
data.description:
• Torrid press release (NYSE:CURV): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.01 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $263.8M (-12.4% Y/Y) misses by $20.49M.

  • For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024 the Company expects:

  • Net sales between $255.0 million and $270.0 million.

  • Adjusted EBITDA between $9.0 million and $15.0 million.

  • For the full year 2024, which has 52 weeks compared to 53 weeks in full year 2023, the Company expects:

  • Net sales between $1.083 billion and $1.098 billion.
  • Adjusted EBITDA between $101.0 million and $107.0 million.
  • Capital expenditures between $20 million and $25 million reflecting infrastructure and technology investments as well as new stores for the year.
